title: Product HUD architecture and diagnostics overlay separation
summary: Make the default player route a playable HUD instead of a diagnostics harness while preserving diagnostics behind explicit dev/debug access.
acceptance: Default UI no longer exposes M6/M8 product-gate, fixture, hash or diagnostics copy as primary player content
Diagnostics remain available only through explicit diagnostics/debug mode and are visibly separated from player HUD
Player HUD exposes current phase, next goal, resources, residents, alerts, selected object and player actions without burying the map
React remains a read-model consumer and issues explicit commands/adapters only; it does not become simulation authority
E2E or unit tests assert default player HUD and explicit diagnostics route behavior

history.details: all dependencies done after parallel WM-0130 and WM-0131 branch integration

Separated default player HUD copy from fixture/product-gate framing, relabeled explicit debug overlay as developer diagnostics, added unit/E2E assertions for default vs wmDiagnostics=1 routes, restored WM-0118 artifact refreshes, and passed all required checks except the known unsupported pnpm test filter mismatch.

verified: No blocking findings; default HUD/debug separation and test assertions confirmed. Reproduced typecheck, handoff validation, task validation, diff check, and quality; required pnpm test filter mismatch reproduced as unsupported.
Verified by Mirror the 4th; integration gates passed in WM-0132 worktree.
